mnemonics.title: My Hen Can't Wait
mnemonics.content: M.H.N.C. - Remember 4 deadlock conditions: Mutual Exclusion, Hold & Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait

mnemonics.title: RAG Cycle Check
mnemonics.content: Resource Allocation Graph → Check cycles → Deadlock Detected. Visualize arrows forming a circle between processes/resources

mnemonics.title: STOP Deadlocks
mnemonics.content: Recovery methods: Select processes to Terminate, Time-out mechanisms, Offer resource Preemption

mnemonics.title: DETECT Steps
mnemonics.content: Deadlock Examination Through Evaluation Cycles Timely: 1. Detect cycles 2. Evaluate state 3. Terminate processes 4. Execute recovery

acronyms.usage: Visual tool to detect deadlock cycles
Entire process flow from identification to resolution

visualTips.visualization: Draw processes as squares and resources as circles. Arrows TO resources = requests, FROM resources = allocations. Circle arrows = deadlock!
Imagine a banker with limited cash (resources). He only approves loans if he can guarantee ALL customers' minimum needs will eventually be met.
Picture processes holding hands in a circle, each waiting for the next to let go. Break the chain to resolve!
Think of a 'Process Graveyard' - terminating selected processes resurrects the system by freeing resources

analogies.analogy: Like a traffic helicopter (OS) spotting a gridlock. Identifies which cars (processes) are blocking each other in circular wait
Similar to a teacher taking away a shared textbook from one student to give to another - temporary 'stealing' to keep class moving
Like unplugging 1 appliance in a circuit overload. Sacrifice one to save the whole system from crashing
